from jupytercards import display_flashcards import csv file = "iaido_points.csv" split_points = [] with open(file, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f: data = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';')) for d in data: #d['back'] = d['back'].replace('\n', '
') for i, line in enumerate(d['back'].split('\n')): split_points.append({'front':d['front'].split()[1].capitalize()+f" | Point {i+1}", 'back':line[3:]}) display_flashcards(split_points, shuffle_cards=True) from jupytercards import display_flashcards import csv file = "iaido_points.csv" split_points = [] with open(file, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f: data = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';')) for d in data: #d['back'] = d['back'].replace('\n', '
') for i, line in enumerate(d['back'].split('\n')): split_points.append({'front':d['front'].split()[1].capitalize()+f" | Point {i+1}", 'back':line[3:]}) display_flashcards(split_points, shuffle_cards=False) Answer Overview Mae Does the performer do enough Sayabiki when they cut the opponent’s face with Nukitsuke? Is the sword taken into Furikaburi with a feeling of thrusting to behind the left ear? Is the tip of the sword above the horizontal position when in Furikaburi? Is the sword brought down without hesitation during Kirioroshi? Is the tip of the sword slightly below horizontal at the end of Kirioroshi? Is the shape and form of Chiburi correct? Is Noto performed correctly? Ushiro When the turn is made, is the left foot moved sufficiently to the front left? Is the horizontal cut made to the opponent’s temple? Ukenagashi When the parry is made, does it protect the upper body? Is the left foot brought back behind the right foot and the cut made along the Kesa line? After the cut has been made, is the left hand in front of the navel and the sword tip a little below horizontal? Tsuka-ate Is the Tsukagashira surely pointed at the opponent’s solar plexus? When the rear opponent is thrust, is this done with the right elbow extended fully and does the left hand bring the Koiguchi to the navel? When the cut is made, is it on the vertical centerline and from the correct position above the head? Kesagiri When the initial upper cut is made, is the right hand above the right shoulder when the sword is rotated? When Chiburi is performed, is it at the correct angle while the person steps back with the left foot at the same time when their left hand takes hold of the Koiguchi? Morotezuki Is the initial cut correctly made from the opponent’s upper head down to their chin when making Nuki Uchi? Does the performer bring their left foot up behind their right? Is Chudan No Kamae correctly made and the sword thrust into the correct target of the body? Is the thrust made with certainty? Does the performer bring their sword above their head in a parrying action when pulling it out from the first opponent? Sanpogiri Is the initial cut to the first opponent made through the correct diagonal angle from the top right side of the head down to the base of the chin? Is the cut to the opponent on the left performed without hesitation? Is the sword brought up to Furikaburi with a parrying action and does the last cut finish at the horizontal? Ganmenate Is the initial strike with the Tsukagashira made between the eyes? When turning to face the opposite direction, is the right hand placed on the hip? When facing the rear opponent, is the body turned completely to the rear with the rear heel slightly raised and in a straight line? Is the thrust performed without too much bend in the knees? Soetezuki When the initial diagonal cut is made from the opponent’s right shoulder down through to the waist, is the right hand at the height of the navel and the sword tip slightly above the horizontal level? Is the sword held securely between the left thumb and forefinger with the right hand near the hip? Does the right hand finish in front of the navel after making the thrust and does the thrusting action adequately reach the opponent’s body? When showing Zanshin, is the right elbow naturally straight and the right hand no higher or lower than the chest level? Shiogiri Is the strike to the first opponent’s hand done firmly and effectively with the flat side of the Tsuka? In making Sayabiki, is the Mune near the Monouchi of the sword on the chest and is the thrust made surely into the solar plexus of the opponent? When the thrust is made, is the left hand brought to the center of the navel and both arms aid the technique with the correct tension? Is the final cut made by going through Waki Gamae without hesitation or pause? Sougiri When the sword is drawn up, is it in a correct position to parry an attack? When moving forwards, does the performer use Okuri Ashi footwork? When making the horizontal cut, is it performed horizontally with the correct angle of the blade? Nukiuchi When the sword is drawn up and out, have both feet moved back adequately to evade the downward cut of the opponent? When the right hand is taken upwards, is it in the center line of the body and is the step forwards with the right foot sufficient to enable the sword to reach the target?

from jupytercards import display_flashcards
kata = ['Mae', 'Ushiro', 'Ukenagashi', 'Tsuka-ate', 'Kesagiri', 'Morotezuki', 'Sanpogiri', 'Ganmenate', 'Soetezuki', 'Shihogiri', 'Sougiri', 'Nukiuchi']

flashcards = []
for i, k in enumerate(kata):
    flashcards.append({"front":k, "back":str(i+1)})
    flashcards.append({"front":str(i+1), "back":k})
    
display_flashcards(flashcards, shuffle_cards=True)









from jupytercards import display_flashcards
kata = ['Mae', 'Ushiro', 'Ukenagashi', 'Tsuka-ate', 'Kesagiri', 'Morotezuki', 'Sanpogiri', 'Ganmenate', 'Soetezuki', 'Shihogiri', 'Sougiri', 'Nukiuchi']

flashcards = []
for i, k in enumerate(kata):
    flashcards.append({"front":k, "back":str(i+1)})
    flashcards.append({"front":str(i+1), "back":k})
    
display_flashcards(flashcards, shuffle_cards=False)









Answer Overview

Mae

Ushiro

Ukenagashi

Tsuka-ate

Kesagiri

Morotezuki

Sanpogiri

Ganmenate

Soetezuki

Shihogiri

Sougiri

Nukiuchi

These flashcards are based on the translations found on the 

Ryoshinkan website, which also includes further explanations of the kata naming, which is interesting and aids in memorization so reading it is recommended.

import csv

file = "iaido_kata_desc.csv"
with open(file, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    data = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
display_flashcards(data, shuffle_cards=True)









import csv

file = "iaido_kata_desc.csv"
with open(file, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    data = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
display_flashcards(data, shuffle_cards=False)









Answer Overview

Mae - Front

Ushiro - Rear

Ukenagashi - Deflection

Tsukaate - Handle strike

Kesagiri - Diagonal cut

Morotezuki - Two-handed thrust

Sanpogiri - Three-way cut

Ganmenate - Face strike

Soetezuki - Joined-hand thrust

Shihogiri - Four-way cut

Sogiri - Complete cutting

Nukiuchi - Sudden draw

Shinpan Shobu consists of a collection of matches and the video will pause just before the flags are shown, giving you the chance to give your desision before being influenced by the official results. The goal is to sharpen your eyes by refereeing along with experienced shinpan.

Play Shinpan Shobu

The matches are selected from the all japan jodo championships 2024 and 2025, all matches have a unanimous winner.

from jupytercards import display_flashcards
import csv

file = "jodo_points_shi.csv"
split_points = []
with open(file, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    data = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
    for d in data:
        for i, line in enumerate(d['back'].split('\n')):
            split_points.append({'front':d['front']+f" | Point {i+1}", 'back':line[3:]})
display_flashcards(split_points, shuffle_cards=True)

from jupytercards import display_flashcards
import csv

file = "jodo_points_uchi.csv"
split_points = []
with open(file, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    data = list(csv.DictReader(f, delimiter=';'))
    for d in data:
        for i, line in enumerate(d['back'].split('\n')):
            split_points.append({'front':d['front']+f" | Point {i+1}", 'back':line[3:]})
display_flashcards(split_points, shuffle_cards=False)









Answer Overview

Tsukizue

Shi:

How is your posture after you have moved your body diagonally backwards and to the right?

Are you striking Uchi’s left wrist after rotating the Jo diagonally upwards and to the right in a large movement?

Are you striking Uchi’s left wrist with a correct Honteuchi?

Uchi:

From a correct Hasso-no-kamae are you entering Shi’s Maai and cutting down to a line parallel to the floor?

Are you adopting the correct Hidari-jodan-no-kamae?

Suigetsu

Shi:

After moving your body diagonally forwards and to the right, are you correctly thrusting Uchi’s Suigetsu by pulling back your left shoulder?

Have you correctly adopted Hikiotoshi-no-kamae?

How is the strength of your Hikiotoshiuchi?

Uchi:

Are you correctly cutting Shi’s Shomen?

After taking sufficient Maai from Hasso-no-kamae are you correctly adopting the Chudan no Kamae?

Hissage

Shi:

Is the Josaki equally aligned with the Tachi in Awase?

From a correct Kuritsuke are you correctly thrusting the Suigetsu?

Uchi:

Is the Tachi’s Kissaki equally aligned with the Jo in Awase?

From Hidari-jodan-no-kamae are you correctly cutting the Shomen?

Have you had Kuritsuke correctly performed on you?

Shamen

Shi:

After moving your body diagonally forwards and to the right, are you correctly dividing the Jo into four equal parts, sliding your right hand and striking Uchi’s temple with the Josaki?

Are you correctly thrusting Uchi’s Suigetsu?

Uchi:

Are you correctly cutting Shomen to a line parallel to the floor?

After taking sufficient Maai are you correctly adopting Hidari-jodan-no-kamae?

Sakan

Shi:

Are you taking an appropriate amount of Maai when you go into Ma Hanmi and move backwards to parry the Kensaki that has been thrust at you?

When you strike the Tachi is your right foot forwards?

Aligning your left foot to your right foot, are you carrying out the Hikiotoshi in a large movement after holding the entire Jo in both hands?

Uchi:

Are you correctly thrusting Shi’s Suigetsu?

After the Tachi is struck and you move backwards, are you doing so with your right, then left and right foot?

Is your Chudan and Hasso-no-kamae correct?

Monomi

Shi:

How is your Ashi Sabaki (footwork)?

Are you striking Uchi’s wrist after rotating the Jo in a large movement?

Are you correctly executing Kaeshizuki?

Uchi:

Are you cutting Shi’s Shomen to a line parallel with the floor?

Have you correctly adopted Hidari-jodan-no-kamae?

Kasumi

Shi:

When you strike in Gyakuteuchi how is your posture and attack?

Are you correctly performing Hikiotoshi-no-kamae and Taiatari?

Uchi:

Stepping forwards with your left then right from a Nisoku-ittou-no-maai are you correctly cutting Shomen?

After having Taiatari performed on you are you correctly moving backwards?

Tachiotoshi

Shi:

After moving your body are you correctly carrying out Gyakuteuchi?

Are you in a correct posture for Kuritsuke?

Uchi:

Are you parrying the Jo after correctly moving your body horizontally to the left?

Are you cutting Shi’s neck after rotating the Tachi above your head in a large movement?

Are you moving backwards correctly after having had Kuritsuke performed on you?

Raiuchi

Shi:

Are you correctly thrusting Uchi’s Suigetsu?

After moving your body diagonally forwards and to the left, are you correctly thrusting Uchi’s Hibara?

Uchi:

Are you correctly cutting Shi’s upper arm?

After taking a large step backwards with your left foot, are you cutting Shi from their shoulder to their neck?

Seigan

Shi:

Are you correctly entering Uchi’s Maai to attack their eyes with the Josaki and then correctly thrusting their Suigetsu?

After moving your body diagonally forwards and to the left, are you correctly thrusting at Uchi’s Hibara?

In response to Uchi’s Katategiri, do you move your wrist out of reach and strike their Suigetsu from a correct Hasso posture?

Uchi:

In the correct Maai relative to Shi how is your posture when you grip the Tsuka with your right hand?

Are you correctly carrying out Katategiri (one handed cut)?

Midaredome

Shi:

When you strike in Gyakuteuchi what is the position of your left hand?

When the Josaki is above your head are you correctly attacking Uchi’s face?

Uchi:

Are you correctly cutting Shi’s Do (upper body)?

After moving your right foot slightly closer to your left foot, do you retaliate by cutting Shi’s Shomen?

Ranai

Shi:

From Hikiotoshi-no-kamae, are you using your left and right feet to sufficiently contain Uchi; are you entering his Temoto?

Are you correctly thrusting Uchi’s Hibara?

After stepping forwards with your right foot, are you immediately cutting Shomen?

Are you moving back correctly in response to Uchi’s attack?

Are you correctly separating from Uchi?

Is your posture on receiving Dobarai correct?

Uchi:

How is your posture after you have drawn the Tachi diagonally upwards and to the right?

Are you correctly cutting Shi’s Do (upper side body)?

After moving your left foot backwards are you correctly parrying the Jo in front of your forehead?

Are you correctly attacking Shi’s chest and cutting it?

After Shi has performed Kurihanashi on you, move backwards using your right, left and right foot, how is your posture and Metsuke?

Are you cutting Shi’s right wrist with Katategiri; and are you stepping forwards and correctly thrusting?

Are you correctly cutting Shi’s Do (upper side body)?

Appendix for Uchi (Tachi)

Be sure to keep your arms and wrists flexible.

How to correctly cut with an effective Hasuji.

from jupytercards import display_flashcards
kata = ['Tsukizue', 'Suigetsu', 'Hissage', 'Shamen', 'Sakan', 'Monomi', 'Kasumi', 'Tachiotoshi', 'Raiuchi', 'Seigan', 'Midaredome', 'Ranai']

flashcards = []
for i, k in enumerate(kata):
    flashcards.append({"front":k, "back":str(i+1)})
    flashcards.append({"front":str(i+1), "back":k})
    
display_flashcards(flashcards, shuffle_cards=True)

Answer Overview

Tsukizue - Walking stick

Suigetsu - Solar plexus

Hissage - Carry in the hands

Shamen - Slope

Sakan - Left thrust

Monomi - Look out

Kasumi - Mist

Tachiotoshi - Drop the sword

Raiuchi - Thunderstrike

Seigan - Straight to the eyes

Midaredome - Preventing chaos

Ranai - From disorder to harmony

hierarchy.lvl1: A collection of digital learning tools
A collection of digital learning tools

records.content:
We are expected to pick up quite a bit of information during practice, such as commands, kata names and terms. Being able to quickly recall these helps free up mental space in the dojo for actual practice. The goal of this website is to help you study at home with classic tools such as flashcards and more experimental tools such as the shinpan practice game.

Senna van Hoek  | Kendo Kai Higashi
A collection of links to other budo related resources on the internet.
Idaho Kendo Club > Iaido Resources | Includes side-by-side translations of various documents including the ZNKR Iai Instruction Manual and ZNKR Iaido Shinpan Rules.

Iaido in Amersfoort | This channel covers a range of subjects such as how to tie your hakama, where the opponents are in kata, and even how to set up competition fields.

All Japan Kendo Federation | Recordings of all ZNKR organised competitions.

Fukuoka Jodo | Recordings of Koryu demonstrations and regional competitions.

All Japan Kendo Federation | Recordings of all ZNKR organised competitions.
Idaho Kendo Club > Kendo Resources | Includes a side-by-side translation of the Kendō Shiai, Shinpan, Adminstration Essentials Guide.

Kendo guide (Hiro) | Kendo training videos for beginners, and tips for the intermediate/advanced to improve our kendo skills.

The Kendo Show | Instructional videos, discussions on the concepts of Kendo, equipment and maintenance information, interviews with other Kendoka, and more!

All Japan Kendo Federation | Recordings of all ZNKR organised competitions.

In essence this website just contains a index of YouTube videos which I have tagged and timestamped with the kata that are shown, I have also added buttons to enable skipping to a given kata.

Mitori Geiko Website

Usefull YouTube shortcuts:While the video is paused, you can use the , and . keys to move a single frame back or forward.
You can use the arrow keys to jump back or forward by 5 seconds.
